articles.title: Judges Visit Rochdale for RHS Competition
articles.excerpt: Judges from the Royal Horticultural Society toured Rochdale, including key sites such as Castleton train station and Denehurst Park, as part of a local competition.
articles.content_html:
Judges from the Royal Horticultural Society (RHS) visited Rochdale on 21 July as part of a local competition. The judges were taken on a route that included notable locations such as Castleton train station, Denehurst Park, and Rochdale town centre.

The visit aimed to assess the town's floral displays and community efforts in horticulture. Local groups, including Rochdale in Bloom, contributed to the preparations for the judges' tour.

The RHS competition encourages towns to enhance their green spaces and promote community involvement in gardening and environmental initiatives. The results of the competition will be announced later this year.

This event highlights Rochdale's commitment to improving its public spaces and fostering community spirit through gardening and environmental stewardship.

articles.title: Scaffolding Project Completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve
articles.excerpt: A scaffolding project has been completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve to facilitate bridge restoration works. The project involved erecting scaffolding for waterproof encapsulation.
articles.content_html:
A scaffolding project has been completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve, Rochdale. The scaffolding was erected to provide a waterproof encapsulation to enable bridge restoration works.

The project highlights the ongoing efforts to maintain and improve local natural sites, ensuring they remain accessible and safe for visitors.

Local contractors undertook the work, which is expected to enhance the structural integrity of the bridge and prolong its lifespan.

The completion of this project may mean improved access for walkers and nature enthusiasts visiting the reserve.

articles.title: Rochdale Weather Forecast: Sunny Intervals Expected This Week
articles.excerpt: Rochdale is set to experience sunny intervals with mild temperatures throughout the week, according to the latest forecast from the Met Office.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale is expected to see sunny intervals this week, with temperatures reaching a maximum of 23 degrees Celsius on Wednesday and Thursday. The forecast indicates that the weather will remain largely dry, with a low chance of rain throughout the week.

Today, temperatures will peak at 23 degrees Celsius, with a minimum of 14 degrees overnight. Winds will be light, with maximum gusts of 9 mph from the southeast. The chance of rain is less than 5%.

Looking ahead, Thursday will also see a high of 23 degrees Celsius, with a 20% chance of rain around midday. Friday's forecast predicts slightly cooler temperatures of 22 degrees Celsius, maintaining similar conditions with sunny intervals.

As the weekend approaches, temperatures are expected to drop to around 20 degrees Celsius on Saturday, with cloudy conditions and a 30% chance of rain in the afternoon. Sunday will see further cooling, with highs of 18 degrees Celsius and light showers likely.

articles.title: Rochdale Council Staff Acknowledged for Their Support
articles.excerpt: Three members of the Environmental Management Department at Rochdale Borough Council received public thanks for their assistance during a recent incident.
articles.content_html:
A post on social media has expressed gratitude towards three members of the Environmental Management Department at Rochdale Borough Council. The staff members, named Julian, Mark, and Brett, were praised for their helpfulness during a recent incident.

The post highlighted how the three staff members 'literally came to our rescue' and noted their kindness and support. The message referred to them as 'absolute stars', reflecting the positive impact of their assistance.

This acknowledgment comes as part of ongoing community engagement, where residents are encouraged to recognise the efforts of local council staff in providing essential services.

Rochdale Borough Council continues to promote a culture of appreciation for its employees, aiming to foster strong relationships between the council and the community.

articles.title: Rochdale In Bloom Welcomes Judges to Denehurst Park
articles.excerpt: Rochdale In Bloom and local teams welcomed judges from North West in Bloom to Denehurst Park as part of their route around the area.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale In Bloom, along with local teams, welcomed judges from North West in Bloom to Denehurst Park on 21 July 2026. The visit is part of a wider route around Rochdale, showcasing the area's floral displays and community efforts in gardening.

The event highlights the ongoing commitment of volunteers and community groups to enhance local green spaces. Denehurst Park, known for its scenic beauty, serves as a focal point for this year's competition.

Participants expressed enthusiasm for the judges' visit, which aims to assess the town's efforts in promoting horticulture and environmental sustainability. The judges will evaluate various aspects of the park and surrounding areas during their tour.

Rochdale In Bloom has been instrumental in fostering community spirit and encouraging residents to take pride in their local environment. The outcome of the judges' assessment will be announced later this year.

articles.title: Healey councillor welcomes first beehive to Number One Riverside roof
articles.excerpt: Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton has welcomed the installation of the first beehive on the roof of Number One Riverside in Rochdale town centre.
articles.summary: A new rooftop beehive supplied and maintained by Boro Bees will make use of the sedum roof and nearby riverbank wildflowers, with its first honey being left for the colony over winter.
articles.content_html:
Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton has welcomed the installation of the first beehive on the roof of Number One Riverside in Rochdale town centre.

The hive was installed with support from Boro Bees, which supplied the colony and will continue to maintain it.

Councillor Ayrton described the installation as exciting news and said the bees would benefit from the building’s sedum roof and the wildflowers growing along the nearby riverbank.

Supporting bees in the town centre

The rooftop location will give the bees access to pollen and nectar from plants around Number One Riverside, the River Roch and areas further afield.

Sedum roofs are planted with low-growing vegetation and can provide additional habitats and food sources for pollinating insects in built-up areas.

The project adds a new biodiversity feature to the council building while demonstrating how urban spaces can be adapted to support wildlife.

First honey to remain with the colony

Any honey produced during the colony’s first year will be left for the bees themselves.

This will help the colony establish itself and provide the food reserves needed to survive and remain healthy through the winter.

Those involved in the project hope the hive may produce surplus honey in future years once the colony is well established.

“The bees will benefit from the sedum roof on the top of Number One Riverside and from the wildflowers that grow along the riverbank.”

Hive to be maintained by local specialists

Boro Bees will be responsible for maintaining the hive and monitoring the health of the colony.

Photographs from the installation show a beekeeper placing frames containing the bees inside the wooden rooftop hive.

This report is based on a public update and photographs published by Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton following the installation of the beehive at Number One Riverside.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports a public update issued by an elected councillor. References to the environmental benefits and future honey production of the hive reflect information shared about the project.

articles.title: Weather Forecast for Castleton: July 16, 2026
articles.excerpt: The weather forecast for Castleton, Rochdale, on July 16, 2026, predicts a mix of sun and clouds with occasional rain showers expected. Temperatures are likely to reach a high of 20 degrees Celsius, while overnight lows may drop to around 12 degrees Celsius.
articles.content_html:
The weather forecast for Castleton, Rochdale, on July 16, 2026, predicts a mix of sun and clouds with occasional rain showers expected.

Temperatures are likely to reach a high of 20 degrees Celsius, while overnight lows may drop to around 12 degrees Celsius.

People should carry umbrellas as rain is expected intermittently throughout the day, particularly in the afternoon.

For those planning travel or outdoor activities, it is advisable to check for updates on weather conditions as they may change.
